finding_results.notes: Replaced summary-only recap with two dramatized beats: Helen overriding Agnes's explicit refusal during speech therapy, and the late-night fentanyl argument where Agnes actively stops a dangerous sale.
finding_results.summary: The chapter now stages both Agnes's dispossession and her ring ethics in live scene pressure instead of relying on dossier-style summary.

finding_results.notes: Harmonized the notebooks with continuity canon across all references: four composition notebooks, with the fourth dedicated to refusals/corrections, hidden under Agnes's mattress and referenced consistently during Carmen's recruitment scene.
finding_results.summary: Notebook count and hiding place are now internally consistent and aligned with the continuity sheet's canonical object state.

finding_results.notes: Revised the Agnes-Carmen reveal scene (lines 155-225) to break up Agnes's polished mini-speeches. Two procedural derails from Carmen now interrupt Agnes before the invitation lands: (1) Carmen's clinical assessment reflex at line 159 ('Since when — is this a neurological change? I need to assess whether —') forces Agnes to clarify the stroke timeline through exchange rather than delivering it as a complete thesis; (2) Carmen's documentation-protocol retreat at line 177 ('I should be notifying the attending. A verbal status change — I'm supposed to document and call —') derails Agnes's reveal and forces a direct refusal. Agnes's buyer-screening role now emerges through Carmen's follow-up questions (line 189: 'What does that mean? What are you tracking?') and her alarmed recognition (line 193: 'You're screening people' / line 197: 'You keep written records. Of all of it. In the building.'). The complicity confrontation (lines 211-219) is now a stepped exchange with Carmen's self-defense and denial ('That's not the same as —' / 'I didn't accommodate anything') rather than Agnes's previous thesis-speech ('That is a real cost. I will not pretend it is not... What you have not done is looked at the work.'). Removed narrator-approval line ('She had made her case. She did not make cases twice.'). Agnes's LOW contraction level preserved throughout; Carmen's HIGH contraction level and trailing-off-under-pressure pattern maintained. All key facts (MMSE score, notebooks, hydroxychloroquine interaction, Carmen's complicity) still land. Notebook setup (agnes_notebooks) preserved with four notebooks mentioned and Agnes pointing toward the bed.

Replaced abstract diagnostic end-stops with concrete institutional residue and sensory follow-through: Helen's visit now leaves Agnes still charted as oriented x0, the placement paragraph ends in an intake-time act of being spoken over, and Carmen's exit resolves in audible med-pass motion rather than paraphrased thematic explanation.
finding_results.summary: The three targeted passage endings now add concrete pressure or afterimage instead of restating the meaning already legible in the scene.

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 8 now dramatizes Agnes's wrongful override and pharmaceutical boundary-setting while preserving the chapter's quiet revelation engine and correcting the notebooks to four composition books hidden under her mattress.

Single finding addressed: broke up Agnes's composed mini-speeches in the Agnes-Carmen reveal scene by adding two procedural derails from Carmen (clinical assessment reflex at line 159, documentation-protocol retreat at line 177) and converting Agnes's thesis-speech turns into stepped exchanges where key facts emerge through Carmen's interruptions, follow-up questions, and self-corrections. Removed the 'That is a real cost' thesis paragraph and the 'What work?' / hydroxychloroquine briefing block, redistributing their information into shorter, pressure-driven exchanges. Contraction levels honor style bible (Agnes LOW, Carmen HIGH). No structural, continuity, or setup changes. Heading contract preserved.
Addressed the prose/copyedit packet by tightening the three flagged endings and preserving the chapter's revelation engine, continuity, and setup obligations.
